title: 第 8 節
en: § 7. Buddhism was brought beyond the Indus as early as Axoka's reign, though it was only under the GraecoBactrian kings (250-125 B.C.) and under the Indo-Grecks (first century before Christ) that it spread widely in the eastern provinces of Iran. One of the greatest IndoGreeks, Menander — Milinda, was revered as a Buddhist saint. In the middle of the first century B.C. Bactriana was famed for its Buddhist priests, the Safxai/aioi, the Shamans. In the first century of our era, Kanishka's coins present, in an instructive eclecticism, all the deities of the Indo-Scythian empire, Greek' gods, Brahmanical devas, Buddha, and the principal yazatas of Mazdeism. If therefore the alleged allusions to Buddhism are accepted, the Avesta passages where they occur cannot have been written earlier than the second century before our era, though they may bear a later date, as Buddhism was uprooted from Eastern Iran only by Islam.

§ 8. We have already seen that Alexander was known to the composer of the H6m Yajt, nay more, that it must be posterior to the fall of the Greek domination in Iran (about 150 B.C.). There was time enough for Greek influence to permeate the Zoroastrian schools, and so it did.

§ 9. The doctrine of the Magi on the duration of the world prevalent during the Achaemenian period is known from Theopompus, a writer contemporary with Philip and Alexander. The existence of the world is divided into periods of three thousand years. During the first two periods Ormazd and Ahriman reign alternately ; during the third period they struggle, and destroy each other s work ; at the end, Ahriman is conquered and men live happily, needing no food and casting no shadow.

This same doctrine is found in Zoroastrian books, but with a characteristic difference. The world lasts four periods of three thousand years each : the third period is filled, as in Theopompus, with the mixture and conflict of the two principles; the fourth period, that opens with the apparition of Zoroaster and the true religion, ends with the ruin of INTRODUCTION, V. Iv Ahriman, the resurrection and future life. But in the first two periods the agreement ceases. In the pre- Alexandrian conception, each period belongs to each of the two principles in turn ; the spirit of the Zoroastrian doctrine is quite mystical During three thousand years the world had only a spiritual^ unseen form, and it remained uncorrupted, unmoving, not perceptible. In the next period of three thousand years, it received material form and began to move, though it was still free from Ahriman.

§ lo. That period of spiritual ideal existence of the world, preceding its material and sensible apparition, reminds one strikingly of the Platonic ideas, and it can hardly have entered Zoroastrianism before Greek philosophy penetrated the East. This hypothesis will seem less bold than it does at first sight, if we remember that, on the confession of old Parsi tradition itself, texts on 'generation and corruption' (yahvQnishn u-vin&sishn), recovered from the Greeks, were embodied in the sacred books as late as the end of the third century of our era ; and that the high-priest Tansar, the man who played so important a part in Ardashtr*s religious revolution, was expressly represented as a member of the Platonic sect. Without pressing conclusions too hard as to facts and dates, this much can be safely inferred from the preceding, that Platonic doctrines had found their way to Persia in the first centuries of the Christian era.

Platonism of course means Neo-Platonism, that is to say that philosophic compound, inspired by the spirit of Plato, which permeated all the speculations of the centuries before Christ and long after, and which finds its first and most influential expression in Philb Judaeus. In Philo is found,* as far as I know, the first exact parallel to the Avesta doctrine mentioned above. As God perceived that no work can be beautiful but from a beautiful model, and that any sensible object needs an ideal archetype, ' when he wanted to create this visible world, he first drew the intelligible one' (fiovXriOels t6v iparov to\)tov\ Koa-fiov brjixiovpyTJa-ai^ irpo€^ervTrov tov roijroj;). The Sparbs KoapLos is the gacthya sti, the vorjTos is the mainyava.
Ivi VENDtDAn.

§ II. The first Genius under Ahura is Vohu Man6, ^ Good Thought/ who is his first spiritual creation and the moving principle of the world. He was created first of all beings ; through him in the beginning Ahura created the world and the religion ; and Ahura takes his advice before proceeding to any of his acts. Besides being his first creation and the instrument of his other creations, he is the type of mankind. At last, in the next world, he is the intercessor between Ahura and man.

When we define Vohu Mand in the words of the Avesta, we define the Logos : and inversely Vohu Mand may be defined in the same terms as Philo's Acfyoy &fios : * as the first manifestation of the divine powers, he is the first-born, the first archangel of God; as an ideal type of human nature, he is the perfect man.' Like Vohu Man6 in the G4thas and still more, the Logos is the instrument of creation. Like him, he is the perfect intercessor, for he applies to the Father to obtain for men the forgiveness of sins and plenty of benefits. As Zarathu.rtra applies to Vohu Man6 for his first instruction, so is the Logos the messenger of God, his elect, the transmitter of his revelations. Both Philo's Logos and the Avesta Vohu Mand are God's first-born and first instrument, the ideal man, the intercessor, the revealer.

§ 12. If Vohu Man6 is a Zoroastrian adaptation of the Logos, it will follow that the Amshaspands themselves are a post-Alexandrian development; for Vohu Man6 is the type of the Amshaspands. As Vohu Mand was chosen to represent mankind, so there grew up round this initial ideal divine abstractions that might be attached, somehow, to the other departments of nature to help like Vohu Mand, and with him, in the creation of the world. This is the series of the six Amesha Spe^tas :
Vohu Man6, Good Thought, reigning over Man (and cattle).
AshaVahixta, Perfect Righteousness, „ Fire.
Khshathra Vairya, Good Royalty, „ Metals.
Spe/fta Armaiti, Pious Modesty, „ Earth.
Haurvat&t, Health, „ Waters.
AmeretAt, Immortality, „ Plants.

Here again Philo presents us with a striking parallel INTRODUCTION, VI. Ivii Between God and the world, the Logos is only the first of a series of divine abstractions or powers (\oyoi, or bvvaficis): in one passage, unfortunately mutilated, he enumerates six of them, the ©cios kSyos being the first. The third, ^ paatkiKrj^ * the Royal virtue/ answers literally to the third member of the Zoroastrian series, Khshathra Vairya. The other members of the Philonian series iroiT/riici}, * the Creative virtue;' tXews, *the powerof Mercy ;' vo^oBtnKri, 'the Legislative virtue/ have no counterpart in the Avesta series, which prevents our attributing any particular historical importance to the coincidence of Khshathra Vairya with the patriXiKri : yet the coincidence is not quite accidental : it was made possible only by the fact that both Philo and the organisers of the Avesta system moved in a common atmosphere of moral and metaphysical abstractions. In fact tXecof, though not one of the Amesha Spefftas, might have become one, and in fact is consecrated and invoked with Khshathra Vairya under the name of Mareirdika^, or in MSLthra Spe«ta, ' the Holy Word,'

This is the Gnostic atmosphere, and the G&thas, which are, on the whole, a poem to the glory of the Amesha Sptntsa and the virtues they impersonate, may be termed the first monument of Gnosticism, but of practical, purely ethic Gnosticism, permeated by a strong sense of reality and a deep pre-occupation with morality : abstraction here is only a means of edification. Philo is nearer the true Gnostics than the writers of the G&thas : they were mere moralists, with no metaphysical instinct.
CHAPTER VL Jewish Elements in the Religion.
§ I. The Jewish influence, less visible in the doctrine than the Greek, is prominent in the general views and the form of the book.
Iviil VENDtoAD.

The Avesta and the Pentateuch are the only two religious books known in which legislation descends from the heavens to the earth in a series of conversations between the lawgiver and his God. Without attaching undue importance to this correspondence, we shall be more impressed with the fact that both books have the same object, viz. to write the history of the creation and mankind ; and in mankind, more especially, the history of the elect race (the Iranians here, the Hebrews there), and in that race the history of the true religion (the religion bf Mazda, revealed by Mazda to Zarathui-tra, and the religion of Jehovah, revealed by Jehovah to Moses). The ultimate end of both books is to teach the faithful the rule of life.
§ a. Here is a series of particular concordances that show more clearly the unity of their plan :

(i) Creation of the world. — Jehovah creates the world in six days ; he creates successively the light, the heaven, the • sea, the earth and the plants, the lights in the firmament, the animals, and lastly man.
Ahura Mazda creates the world in six periods ; he creates successively the heaven, the water, the earth, the plants, the animals, and man.

(a) Creation of man. — All the human race, in Genesis, is descended from one couple, man and woman, Adam and Eve (Adam means * man *)* All the human race, in the Avesta, is descended from one couple, man and woman, Mashya and Mashyana (Mashya means * man ').

(3) The Deluge. — Jehovah intends to destroy the human race, on account of its wickedness, and to renew it. He brings about the deluge, but saves one just man, Noah, with his family and a couple of each species of animals. Noah, on his advice, builds an ark, in which he takes refuge, with his people, and from which he goes out afterwards to repeople the earth.

In course of time, the earth shall be laid waste by the snows and rains of three long winters, the Mahrkfisha winters. Ahura, in order to repeople his earth with superior races, orders kind Yima to build an underground INTRODUCTION, VI. lix palace, the Var of Yima, where the finest specimens of human, animal, and vegetable species will live till the moment when, the evil days being over, they shall open the doors of the Var and repeople the earth with a better race, (4) Division of the Earth. — Noah has three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japhet, the ancestors of the three races between which the earth is divided.

Thra^taona, the avenger and successor of Yima, has three sons, Airya, Sairima, and Tdra, between whom the earth is divided : Airya receives Iran, the centre of the earth's surface, Sairima receives the West, and Tfira the East.

Putting aside the legend of Airya, killed by his brothers, which reminds one, but not closely enough, of Joseph persecuted by his brethren, we an'ive at the fact that is the central interest of the two books :
zh: § 7. 佛教在阿育王（Axoka）統治時期已傳入印度河外，但直到希臘巴克特里亞諸王（西元前250-125年）統治下，以及在印度—希臘人統治時期（基督前第一世紀），它才在伊朗東部各省廣泛傳播。最偉大的印度—希臘人之一米南德——彌林陀（Menander — Milinda），被尊為佛教聖人。在西元前第一世紀中期，巴克特里亞因其佛教僧侶——薩克迦人（Safxai/aioi）、薩滿教士而聞名。在我們時代的第一世紀，迦尼色迦（Kanishka）的錢幣展現了一種富有啟發意義的折衷主義，呈現了印度—斯基泰帝國的所有神祇：希臘諸神、婆羅門提婆、佛陀，以及祆教的主要雅茲塔（yazatas）。因此，若接受所謂關於佛教的典故，那麼《阿維斯塔》（Avesta）中出現這些典故的章節不可能在我們時代前第二世紀之前撰寫，儘管它們的日期可能較晚，因為佛教僅由伊斯蘭教將其從東伊朗根除。
§ 8. 我們已經看到，作曲家在《霍姆雅茲特》(Hôm Yajt) 中已知道亞歷山大的事蹟，更進一步說，這部著作必然成書於希臘統治在伊朗衰落之後（大約西元前150年）。有足夠的時間讓希臘文明的影響滲透入祆教學派，而事實上它確實做到了。
§ 9. 關於瑣羅亞斯德教士（Magi）在阿契美尼德時期流行的世界延續說法，我們從與腓力和亞歷山大同時代的史家西奧龐皮烏斯（Theopompus）的著作中得知。世界的存在被分為三千年的週期。在前兩個週期中，歐瑪茲德（Ormazd）與阿赫里曼（Ahriman）輪流統治；在第三個週期中，他們相互爭鬥，摧毀彼此的事業；到了末期，阿赫里曼被征服，人類幸福地生活著，不需要食物，也不投下陰影。

這同樣的教義見於瑪雅斯德教典籍中，但具有一個特徵性的差異。世界經歷四個時期，每個時期三千年：第三個時期如同在西奧龐帕斯那樣，充滿了兩個原理的混合與衝突；第四個時期自瑪雅斯德的顯現與真正的宗教開始，以阿里曼的毀滅、死者的復活及來世而告終。然而在最初的兩個時期，一致性便告中斷。在前亞歷山大時期的理解中，每個時期依次屬於兩個原理中的各一個；瑪雅斯德教義的精神完全是神秘的。三千年間，世界僅具有靈性的、無形的形式，並保持著不被腐蝕、不動搖、無法感知的狀態。在隨後的三千年時期，它獲得了物質形式並開始運動，儘管它仍然免於阿里曼的侵害。

§ 10. 世界在物質及感知的出現之前所經歷的那個精神理想存在時期，令人驚人地想起柏拉圖的理型說，而且它幾難在希臘哲學滲透東方之前就進入拜火教。若我們回想起古代波斯傳統本身所承認的事實，即關於「生成與毀壞」（*yahvQnishn u-vinsishn*）的文本乃是從希臘人那裡尋獲，並在我們紀年第三世紀末時才被編入聖書之中；而且在阿爾達希爾（*Ardashir*）宗教革命中扮演如此重要角色的大祭司坦薩爾（*Tansar*），則明確地被記述為柏拉圖派的一員——如此看來，這個假說就不會顯得如初時那般大膽。儘管我們不應過度推衍關乎事實與年代的結論，但從以上各點還是可以穩妥地推斷出：柏拉圖的教說在基督教紀年的最初幾個世紀已經傳入波斯。

柏拉圖主義當然是指新柏拉圖主義，亦即那種受柏拉圖精神啟發的哲學綜合體，它貫穿了基督之前及其後長期的所有思辨，並在斐洛‧猶太人（Philo Judaeus）身上首次找到最具影響力的表現。據我所知，在斐洛著作中可以找到與上述阿維斯陀（Avesta）教義最初的精確對應。當上帝察覺到沒有美麗的模型就不能有美麗的作品，且任何感性的對象都需要一個理想的原型時，當他想要創造這個可見的世界時，他首先勾畫了那個可知的世界（βουληθεὶς τὸν ἀρσον τοῦτον κόσμον δημιουργῆσαι προεξετύπωσεν τὸν τούτου）。那個感性的宇宙（σαρκὸς κόσμος）是*gacthya sti*，那個可知的（νοητὸς）是*mainyava*。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」只有「Ivi VENDtDAn」，這似乎不是英文句子，可能是： 1. 掃描/OCR 誤讀 2. 不完整的文本片段 3. 標題或專有名詞 為了提供準確的翻譯，請確認並提供完整的英文原文。我已準備好按照您的規則進行翻譯。
§ II. 阿胡拉之下的第一位神靈是沃胡‧曼（Vohu Man），即「善念」，他是阿胡拉的第一個精神造物，亦是世界的推動原則。他乃是萬物中最先被創造者；在太初之時，阿胡拉藉由他創造了世界與宗教；阿胡拉在進行任何行為之前都會諮詢他的意見。除了作為他的第一個造物和其他造物的工具之外，他還是人類的原型。最後，在來世中，他是阿胡拉與人類之間的調停者。

當我們用《阿維斯陀》的措辭來定義沃胡‧曼德（Vohu Mand）時，我們就是在定義邏各斯（Logos）；反之亦然，沃胡‧曼德也可以用與斐洛的邏各斯（Acfyoy &fios）相同的詞彙來定義：*作為神聖力量的首次顯現，他是首生者，上帝的第一位大天使；作為人性的理想典型，他是完美人類。' 正如《迦陀經》中的沃胡‧曼德，更是如此，邏各斯是創造的工具。如同他一樣，他是完美的代禱者，因為他向父祈求，以為人類獲得罪惡的寬恕和眾多福祉。正如瑣羅亞斯德向沃胡‧曼德尋求他的首次啟蒙，邏各斯也是上帝的使者、他的選民、他啟示的傳達者。斐洛的邏各斯與阿維斯陀的沃胡‧曼德都是上帝的首生者和首要工具、理想人類、代禱者、啟示者。
§ 12. 若沃胡‧曼努（Vohu Manô）乃瑣羅亞斯德教對邏各斯（Logos）之改編，則阿姆沙斯班德本身亦為亞歷山大之後之發展；蓋沃胡‧曼努乃阿姆沙斯班德之原型。如沃胡‧曼努被選以代表人類，則環繞此初始之理想而產生諸種提婆抽象概念，該等概念得以某種方式關聯於自然之各部門，以助沃胡‧曼努與之共同創造世界。此即六位阿美沙斯畢塔（Amesha Spe^tas）之序列：
沃胡·曼那（Vohu Manó）﹐善念之神﹐統治人類（及牲畜）。
阿沙‧瓦希什塔（Asha Vahixta），完美的正義，「火」。
克沙特拉‧韋瑞亞（Khshathra Vairya），美好的王權，「金屬。
斯佩塔‧阿爾邁蒂（Speṭa Armaiti），虔敬謙遜，「大地」。
豪爾瓦塔（Haurvat&t）、健康、「水」。
阿美瑞特（Ameretāt），不朽性，植物。

這裡菲洛（Philo）再次為我們呈現了一個引人注目的類比。在上帝與世界之間，邏各斯（Logos）只是一系列神聖的抽象概念或力量（*λόγοι*或*δυνάμεις*）中的第一個：在一段遺憾地殘缺的文段中，他列舉了其中的六個，*ὁ θεῖος λόγος*為首位。第三個，*ἡ βασιλικὴ*，「王的德行」，在字面上對應於瑣羅亞斯德系列中的第三個成員，**赫沙特拉‧瓦伊利亞**（Khshathra Vairya）。菲洛系列中的其他成員——*ποιητική*，「創造的德行」；*ἔλεος*，「慈悲的力量」；*νομοθετική*，「立法的德行」——在《阿維斯陀》系列中沒有對應項，這使得我們無法將*βασιλικὴ*與赫沙特拉‧瓦伊利亞的巧合歸因於任何特殊的歷史重要性：然而，這種巧合並非完全偶然，它之所以成為可能，僅因於菲洛和《阿維斯陀》系統的組織者都處於道德與形而上學抽象概念的共同氛圍中。事實上，*ἔλεος*雖然不是**不死聖靈**（Amesha Spentas）之一，但本可成為其中之一，而且實際上被尊聖化並與赫沙特拉‧瓦伊利亞一起祈禱，稱為**馬雷爾迪卡**（Mareirdika）或**密特拉‧史彼塔**（Mithra Spenta），「聖言」。
這正是靈知主義的氛圍，而《迦陀》（G&thas）作為整體而言是一首讚頌不死聖靈（Amesha Sptntsa）及其所體現之美德的詩篇，可謂靈知主義的第一座紀念碑，然其為實踐性、純粹倫理性的靈知主義，充溢著強烈的現實感與對道德的深切關懷：此處抽象概念僅作為道德教化的手段。斐洛比《迦陀》的撰者更接近真正的靈知主義者：他們不過是道德家，毫無形上學的直覺。
§ I. 猶太人的影響雖然在教義中不如希臘影響那般顯著，但在本書的總體觀點和形式中卻是突出的。
我已經準備好進行翻譯，但發現您提供的「英文原文」為： **Iviil VENDtoAD.** 這段文字似乎不是有效的英文內容，可能是： - 掃描或輸入錯誤 - 編碼問題導致亂碼 請您確認並提供正確的英文原文，我將立即按照您的規則進行專業的繁體中文翻譯。

《阿維斯塔》（Avesta）和《五經》是僅有的兩部宗教經典，其中立法透過立法者與其上帝之間一系列的對話，從天堂降臨到地上。雖然不應過度強調這種對應關係，但我們會對這樣一個事實印象更深刻：兩部經典有著相同的目的，即記述創造和人類的歷史；在人類當中，尤其是記述被選中種族的歷史（此處為伊朗人，彼處為希伯來人），以及在該種族中真正宗教的歷史（馬茲達的宗教，由馬茲達啟示予瑣羅亞斯德，以及耶和華的宗教，由耶和華啟示予摩西）。兩部經典的最終目的都是教導信徒生活的法則。
§ a. 茲列舉一系列特殊的相應現象，更清楚地顯示其計畫之統一性：
（一）世界的創造。——耶和華在六日內創造了世界；祂依次創造了光、天、海、地與植物、天穹中的光體、動物，最後創造了人。
阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）分六個時期創造世界；他依次創造了天穹、水、大地、植物、動物和人類。
（甲）人類的創造。——《創世記》中，全人類均源自一對人類和女性，亞當和夏娃（亞當意為「人」）。《阿維斯陀》中，全人類均源自一對人類和女性，瑪什亞和瑪什亞娜（瑪什亞意為「人」）。
（三）洪水泛濫。——耶和華由於人類的邪惡而決意摧毀人類，並重新造就人類。祂引發洪水泛濫，但拯救了一位義人挪亞及其家族，以及每一物種的一對動物。挪亞按照祂的告誡建造方舟，與他的人民一同躲避其中，然後從方舟中出來，重新在地上繁衍人類。
在漫長的歲月中，地球將被三個長期冬季——馬赫克菲沙冬季的冰雪所摧毀。為了用優越的種族重新充實他的地球，阿胡拉命令仁慈的伊瑪建造一座地下宮殿，即伊瑪的瓦爾（Var），其中將住著人類、動物和植物種屬的最優良標本，直到邪惡的日子結束之時，他們才會開啟瓦爾的大門，並用更優越的種族重新充實地球。(4) 地球的分配。——挪亞有三個兒子：閃、含和雅弗，他們是三個種族的祖先，地球在這三個種族之間被分割。
特拉塔歐納（Thra^taona）是伊瑪的復仇者與繼承人，他有三個兒子：艾爾亞、塞伊瑞瑪與圖拉，他們分割了大地：艾爾亞得到伊朗（大地表面的中心），塞伊瑞瑪得到西方，圖拉得到東方。
撇開艾里亞（Airya）遭兄弟所殺的傳說不論——這個故事令人想起約瑟遭兄弟迫害的故事，但相似之處並不十分緊密——我們抵達了這兩部著作的中心關切所在的事實：
